tools.name: context
tools.description: Get API domain context: resource taxonomy, auth requirements, query tips, and unique capabilities. Call this first.

tools.name: export
tools.description:
Export paginated API data to a local file. Supports JSONL (one JSON object
per line, streaming-friendly) and JSON (array). JSONL is recommended for
large datasets as it has no memory pressure.

args.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

format.description: Output format: jsonl or json (default: jsonl)

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

no-cache.description: Bypass response cache for fresh data (default: false)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: fedex_execute
tools.description: Execute one fedex API endpoint by its endpoint_id (from fedex_search). Params are passed as a JSON object; path placeholders and query strings are resolved automatically.

params.description: Parameters for the endpoint. Path placeholders match by name; remaining entries become query string on GET/DELETE or JSON body on POST/PUT/PATCH.

tools.name: fedex_search
tools.description: Search the fedex API for endpoints matching a natural-language query. Returns a ranked list of {endpoint_id, method, path, summary} entries. Call this first to find the endpoint to execute.

query.description: Natural-language description of what you want to do.

tools.name: import
tools.description:
Import data from a JSONL file by issuing POST requests for each record.
Each line must be a valid JSON object. Failed records are logged to stderr
but do not stop the import.

args.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

batch-size.description: Records per batch (future: batch API support) (default: 1)

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

dry-run.description: Preview import without sending requests (default: false)

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

input.description: Input JSONL file path (use - for stdin)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

quiet.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

rate-limit.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: manifest
tools.description:
Builds a markdown manifest of every shipment created on the given day from the local
archive. The text/markdown report has the same content a PDF would; PDF generation
requires external tooling and is not bundled. With --close, also calls the FedEx
end-of-day endpoint to formally close the manifest.

close.description: Also call /ship/v1/endofday/ to close the manifest (default: false)

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

date.description: Day to summarize (YYYY-MM-DD or today) (default: today)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

output.description: Write the manifest to this path (default stdout)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

quiet.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

rate-limit.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: rate_shop
tools.description:
Quote rates across every applicable service type for a single shipment, persist
each quote to the local rate_quotes ledger, and rank by net cost or transit days.
The selected (cheapest, when ranking by cost) quote is marked selected=1 in the
ledger so spend/lane reports can attribute follow-on shipments back to the chosen rate.

account.description: FedEx account number (defaults to env FEDEX_ACCOUNT_NUMBER)

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

quiet.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

rank-by.description: Ranking field: cost or transit (default: cost)

rate-limit.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

services.description: Comma-separated service types to consider when ranking (default: FEDEX_GROUND,FEDEX_2_DAY,STANDARD_OVERNIGHT,PRIORITY_OVERNIGHT,FEDEX_EXPRESS_SAVER,GROUND_HOME_DELIVERY)

weight.description: Package weight (e.g. 5, 5lb, 2.5kg). When the value carries a unit suffix it overrides --units.

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: ship_bulk
tools.description:
Read a CSV of orders, ship each row through FedEx, write per-row results to a ledger
CSV (PASS/FAIL plus tracking number and net charge), and persist successful shipments
to the local archive. Optionally save labels as PDFs. Resumable via --resume which skips
rows whose order_id is already in the archive (matched on the reference column).

account.description: FedEx account number (defaults to env FEDEX_ACCOUNT_NUMBER)

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

from-country.description: Shipper country code (defaults to env FEDEX_SHIPPER_COUNTRY or US)

from-name.description: Shipper contact name (defaults to env FEDEX_SHIPPER_NAME)

from-state.description: Shipper state code (defaults to env FEDEX_SHIPPER_STATE)

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

output.description: Directory to write label PDFs (none if empty)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

quiet.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

rate-limit.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

resume.description: Skip rows already shipped (matched by reference) (default: false)

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

service.description: Default service type when row's service_type is empty (default: FEDEX_GROUND)

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: ship_etd
tools.description:
Single-command Electronic Trade Documents shipping. Reads a commercial-invoice PDF
from disk, base64-encodes it into the shipment request as an embedded ETD document,
and creates the international shipment in one call. Note: this is the simplified flow —
the upstream FedEx documents/upload endpoint is not bundled in this spec, so the invoice
is embedded inline rather than uploaded separately.

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

currency.description: Currency for customs and unit price (default: USD)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)

profile.description: Apply values from a saved profile (see 'fedex-pp-cli profile list')

quiet.description: Bare output, one value per line (default: false)

rate-limit.description: Max requests per second (0 to disable) (default: 0)

select.description: Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

weight.description: Package weight (e.g. 2, 2kg, 5lb). When the value carries a unit suffix it overrides --units.

yes.description: Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts) (default: false)

tools.name: tail
tools.description:
Tail streams live data changes by polling the API at configurable intervals.
Events are emitted as NDJSON to stdout for piping to other tools.
Gracefully shuts down on SIGTERM/SIGINT.

Note: For APIs with WebSocket or SSE support, a future version will use
native streaming instead of polling.

args.description: Additional positional arguments or raw CLI flags to append to the command.

compact.description: Return only key fields (id, name, status, timestamps) for minimal token usage (default: false)

csv.description: Output as CSV (table and array responses) (default: false)

data-source.description: Data source for read commands: auto (live with local fallback), live (API only), local (synced data only) (default: auto)

deliver.description: Route output to a sink: stdout (default), file:<path>, webhook:<url>

follow.description: Keep running (set --follow=false for single poll) (default: true)

human-friendly.description: Enable colored output and rich formatting (default: false)

no-input.description: Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents) (default: false)

plain.description: Output as plain tab-separated text (default: false)
